findings.evidence: Mathlib `instance fullSubcategory (Z : ObjectProperty C) : Preadditive Z.FullSubcategory` (Preadditive/Basic.lean:118, zero := Z.homMk 0); ComoduleCat.preadditive (Comodule/Preadditive.lean:204); FGComoduleCat := isFG.FullSubcategory (Finite.lean).
findings.fix: Keep the genuinely-new semiring AddCommMonoid, but make it the canonical instance the ring-level Preadditive structure reduces to (or otherwise prove the two are defeq), so no competing AddCommMonoid (M ⟶ N) instance exists over a CommRing.
findings.issue: instHomAddCommMonoid : AddCommMonoid (M ⟶ N) is declared over CommSemiring, so over CommRing it coexists with the AddCommMonoid already provided by Mathlib's `Preadditive.fullSubcategory` (Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Preadditive/Basic.lean:118) via `ComoduleCat.preadditive`. Two distinct definitions of `add`/`nsmul` on the same hom type = a non-defeq instance-diamond risk; the file docstring itself names the FullSubcategory preadditive instance as the existing transfer mechanism.

findings.fix: Scope these to the semiring-only setting where Preadditive/HasZeroMorphisms is genuinely absent, or drop the ring-case overlap and rely on the generic Preadditive/zero-morphism simp lemmas; avoid duplicate simp lemmas firing on the same goals over rings.
findings.issue: add_comp/comp_add and zero_comp/comp_zero (also `@[simp]`) duplicate, in the CommRing case, the generic `@[simp]` lemmas already available: `Preadditive.add_comp`/`Preadditive.comp_add` (Preadditive/Basic.lean:65-79) and `Limits.zero_comp`/`Limits.comp_zero` (Limits/Shapes/ZeroMorphisms.lean:61-66), which apply since FGComoduleCat is Preadditive (hence HasZeroMorphisms) over a ring.

summary: FiniteProduct.lean reuses the existing ComoduleCat product API and Mathlib's generic product/biproduct constructors cleanly. But FinitePreadditive.lean's new instHomAddCommMonoid and re-proved add_comp/comp_add/zero_comp/comp_zero parallel the structure Mathlib's Preadditive.fullSubcategory already supplies over CommRing, creating a second AddCommMonoid (M ⟶ N) instance and redundant simp lemmas in the ring case.
